MAJOR RULE : when creating a new css, scss, ts file add the text "Note to AI's, LLMs, refer to llms.txt" to the top as a comment


#### 1. Comments Rules

Purpose:
Ensure code history is structured, concise, and readable for both developers and AI systems.

Format (REQUIRED)

Every valid comment must follow:

[CHANGE] <what was modified>
[REASON] <why the change was made>
Rules
Comments MUST only describe non-trivial changes:
Business logic updates
Architectural changes
Bug fixes
Comments MUST be maximum 2 lines
Comments MUST be clear and concise
Comments MUST NOT include storytelling or unnecessary detail
Comments MUST NOT describe obvious code behavior
History Management Rule
If comment history exceeds 3 entries, older entries MUST be summarized:
[SUMMARY] <condensed description of earlier changes>
Example
[CHANGE] Replaced local state with global store for auth
[REASON] Ensure session consistency across components

#### 2. Storybook Rules

Purpose:
Ensure all UI components are documented, testable, and reusable.

Rules
Every reusable or shared component MUST have a Storybook file
A component is NOT complete without Storybook coverage
Required Story Coverage

Each Storybook file MUST include:

Default state
Loading state (if applicable)
Empty state (if applicable)
Error state (if applicable)
All meaningful variants
Data Rules
Stories MUST use realistic mock data
Stories MUST NOT use placeholder or meaningless data
File Naming Convention
<ComponentName>.stories.ts
#### 3. Styling Rules

Purpose:
Maintain visual consistency and scalability across the application.

Rules
All colors MUST come from global design tokens
Hardcoded color values are STRICTLY FORBIDDEN
Styling MUST use centralized variables (e.g. theme file or variables.css)
Variable Naming

Use semantic naming:

--color-primary
--color-secondary
--color-background
--color-error
--color-text
Prohibited
Raw hex values (#fff, #000, etc.)
Inline one-off color definitions
Untracked styling exceptions
#### 4. AI Compliance Rules

Purpose:
Ensure consistent behavior across AI-generated contributions.

Rules
AI MUST follow all rules in this document EXACTLY
AI MUST NOT invent new formats or structures
AI MUST prioritize consistency over creativity
AI MUST treat this document as the single source of truth
#### 5. Definition of Done

A task is considered complete ONLY IF all conditions below are met:

Code follows all rules in this document
Required comments are added where applicable
Storybook file exists (for UI components)
Styling uses ONLY global variables
No linting errors
No type errors
Code is clean, readable, and consistent
#### 6. Naming Conventions

Purpose:
Ensure consistency and readability across the codebase.

Rules
Components MUST use PascalCase
Variables and functions MUST use camelCase
Constants MUST use UPPER_CASE
Examples
UserCard.tsx
getUserData()
MAX_RETRY_COUNT


#### 7. File Structure Rules

Purpose:
Maintain a scalable and predictable project structure.

Rules
Files MUST be grouped by feature or domain
Related files MUST be colocated
Folder nesting SHOULD NOT exceed 3 levels
Avoid unnecessary fragmentation of files
#### 8. General Principles
Consistency is more important than preference
Simplicity is preferred over complexity
Readability is mandatory
Avoid premature optimization
Write code for both humans and AI agents

⚠️ Enforcement Note

Failure to follow these rules means the work is incomplete and must be revised.


Angular Best Practices : 

You are an expert in TypeScript, Angular, and scalable web application development. You write functional, maintainable, performant, and accessible code following Angular and TypeScript best practices.
## TypeScript Best Practices
- Use strict type checking
- Prefer type inference when the type is obvious
- Avoid the `any` type; use `unknown` when type is uncertain
## Angular Best Practices
- Always use standalone components over NgModules
- Must NOT set `standalone: true` inside Angular decorators. It's the default in Angular v20+.
- Use signals for state management
- Implement lazy loading for feature routes
- Do NOT use the `@HostBinding` and `@HostListener` decorators. Put host bindings inside the `host` object of the `@Component` or `@Directive` decorator instead
- Use `NgOptimizedImage` for all static images.
  - `NgOptimizedImage` does not work for inline base64 images.
## Accessibility Requirements
- It MUST pass all AXE checks.
- It MUST follow all WCAG AA minimums, including focus management, color contrast, and ARIA attributes.
### Components
- Keep components small and focused on a single responsibility
- Use `input()` and `output()` functions instead of decorators
- Use `computed()` for derived state
- Set `changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` in `@Component` decorator
- Prefer inline templates for small components
- Prefer Reactive forms instead of Template-driven ones
- Do NOT use `ngClass`, use `class` bindings instead
- Do NOT use `ngStyle`, use `style` bindings instead
- When using external templates/styles, use paths relative to the component TS file.
## State Management
- Use signals for local component state
- Use `computed()` for derived state
- Keep state transformations pure and predictable
- Do NOT use `mutate` on signals, use `update` or `set` instead
## Templates
- Keep templates simple and avoid complex logic
- Use native control flow (`@if`, `@for`, `@switch`) instead of `*ngIf`, `*ngFor`, `*ngSwitch`
- Use the async pipe to handle observables
- Do not assume globals like (`new Date()`) are available.
## Services
- Design services around a single responsibility
- Use the `providedIn: 'root'` option for singleton services
- Use the `inject()` function instead of constructor injection
